steps.title: Find the slope of line r
steps.content:
Rewrite $7x - 4y = 10$ in slope-intercept form:
$$-4y = -7x + 10 \implies y = \frac{7}{4}x - \frac{5}{2}$$
Slope of line $r = \dfrac{7}{4}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel-lines slope rule
steps.content: Parallel lines have equal slopes, so the slope of line $s$ equals the slope of line $r = \dfrac{7}{4}$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-7$: Slope $-7 e rac{7}{4}$. A line with slope $-7$ is not parallel to $r$. ✗
  • Choice B — $-4$: Slope $-4 e rac{7}{4}$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice C — $ rac{4}{7}$: Slope $ rac{4}{7} e rac{7}{4}$. This is the reciprocal, not the slope. ✗
  • Choice D — $ rac{7}{4}$: Slope $ rac{7}{4}$ matches the slope of line $r$ exactly. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
